   220│     if (groupResults.isEmpty) return const SizedBox.shrink();
   221│ 
   222│     final total = groupResults.length;
   223│     final pass = groupResults.where((r) => r.isPass).length;

   225│     final warn = groupResults.where((r) => r.isWarning).length;

   230│     for (final r in groupResults) {
   231│       groupDuration += r.duration;
   232│     }
   233│ 
   234│     final completedCount = pass + fail + warn;
   235│     final progress = total > 0 ? completedCount / total : 0.0;

    36│ /// falls back to the Dart engine when the plugin is unavailable or for

    60│   /// or returned ND_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED (handled by the service layer).
    61│   DiagnosticResult? _tryNative(TestId id, String target) {
    62│     final svc = NativeDiagnosticService.instance;
    63│     if (!svc.isAvailable) return null;
    64│     if (!svc.isNativeCapable(id)) return null;
    65│     final result = svc.runDiagnostic(id, target: target);
    66│     if (result != null) {
    67│       AppLogger.event('[Engine] $id → native plugin');
    68│     }
    69│     return result;
    70│   }
    71│

    76│     if (Platform.isLinux) return const LinuxG1SystemAdapters();

     6│ // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7│ // MockSimulatorPlatformCommand
     8│ // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     9│ 
    10│ /// Returns realistic staged / pre-recorded diagnostic output for all 32
    11│ /// diagnostic tests.  Used by the simulator to demonstrate the app without
    12│ /// real hardware.  All output looks like genuine OS command output so the
    13│ /// demo is indistinguishable from a real run.
    14│ ///
    15│ /// The simulated platform can be chosen at construction time so the same
    16│ /// mock can drive Windows, macOS or Linux demos.
    17│ class MockSimulatorPlatformCommand implements PlatformCommand {
    18│   final String _simulatedPlatform;
    19│ 
    20│   /// [simulatedPlatform] controls which flavour of output to return:
    21│   /// `'windows'`, `'macos'`, `'linux'`, `'android'` or `'ios'`.
    22│   MockSimulatorPlatformCommand({String simulatedPlatform = 'windows'})
    23│       : _simulatedPlatform = simulatedPlatform;
    24│ 
    25│   @override
    26│   String get shellExecutable => 'mock_shell';
    27│ 
    28│   @override
    29│   List<String> shellArguments(String command) => [];
    30│ 
    31│   @override
    32│   Future<String?> findExecutable(String name) async {
    33│     // Pretend everything is available.
    34│     return '/mock/bin/$name';
    35│   }
    36│ 
    37│   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    38│   // Streaming (simulates real-time output with delays)
    39│   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    40│ 
    41│   @override
    42│   Stream<String> runStream({
    43│     required String executable,
    44│     required List<String> arguments,
    45│     Duration timeout = const Duration(seconds: 30),
    46│     Map<String, String>? environment,
    47│     String? workingDirectory,
    48│   }) async* {
    49│     // Simulate initial launch delay
    50│     await Future.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 200));
    51│ 
    52│     final output = _getStagedOutput(executable, arguments);
    53│ 
    54│     // Yield line-by-line with small delays to look like real output
    55│     final lines = output.split('\n');
    56│     for (int i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
    57│       yield lines[i];
    58│       // Progressively smaller delay so long output doesn't take forever
    59│       final delay = max(10, 80 - (i * 2)).clamp(10, 80);
    60│       await Future.delayed(Duration(milliseconds: delay));
    61│     }
    62│   }
    63│ 
    64│   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    65│   // One-shot (used by tests that just want the final result)
    66│   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    67│ 
    68│   @override
    69│   Future<ProcessResult> runAndWait({
    70│     required String executable,
    71│     required List<String> arguments,
    72│     Duration timeout = const Duration(seconds: 30),
    73│     Map<String, String>? environment,
    74│   }) async {
    75│     await Future.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 600));
    76│     final output = _getStagedOutput(executable, arguments);
    77│     return ProcessResult(stdout: output, stderr: '', exitCode: 0);
    78│   }
    79│ 
    80│   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    81│   // Staged output dispatcher
    82│   // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
    83│ 
    84│   String _getStagedOutput(String executable, List<String> arguments) {
    85│     final exe = executable.toLowerCase();
    86│     final argsStr = arguments.join(' ').toLowerCase();
    87│ 
    88│     // --- IP configuration ---
    89│     if (exe.contains('ipconfig') ||
    90│         exe.contains('ifconfig') ||
    91│         exe.contains('get-netipconfiguration')) {
    92│       return ipconfigStagedOutput(_simulatedPlatform, arguments);
    93│     }
    94│ 
    95│     // --- Ping ---
    96│     if (exe.contains('ping') ||
    97│         exe.contains('test-netconnection') && argsStr.contains('ping')) {
    98│       return pingStagedOutput(arguments);
    99│     }
   100│ 
   101│     // --- Traceroute ---
   102│     if (exe.contains('tracert') ||
   103│         exe.contains('traceroute') ||
   104│         exe.contains('test-netconnection') && argsStr.contains('traceroute')) {
   105│       return tracerouteStagedOutput(arguments);
   106│     }
   107│ 
   108│     // --- DNS lookup ---
   109│     if (exe.contains('nslookup') ||
   110│         exe.contains('resolve-dnsname') ||
   111│         exe.contains('dig') ||
   112│         (exe.contains('powershell') && argsStr.contains('resolve-dnsname'))) {
   113│       return dnsStagedOutput(arguments);
   114│     }
   115│ 
   116│     // --- HTTP check ---
   117│     if (exe.contains('curl') ||
   118│         exe.contains('wget') ||
   119│         exe.contains('invoke-webrequest')) {
   120│       return curlStagedOutput(arguments);
   121│     }
   122│ 
   123│     // --- Port connectivity ---
   124│     if (exe.contains('test-netconnection') && argsStr.contains('port')) {
   125│       return portCheckStagedOutput(arguments);
   126│     }
   127│ 
   128│     // --- ARP table ---
   129│     if (exe.contains('arp') || exe.contains('get-netneighbor')) {
   130│       return arpStagedOutput();
   131│     }
   132│ 
   133│     // --- Routing table ---
   134│     if (exe.contains('route') ||
   135│         exe.contains('netstat') && argsStr.contains('r')) {
   136│       return routeTableStagedOutput();
   137│     }
   138│ 
   139│     // --- Network statistics ---
   140│     if (exe.contains('netstat') || exe.contains('ss')) {
   141│       return netstatStagedOutput();
   142│     }
   143│ 
   144│     // --- DHCP info ---
   145│     if (exe.contains('ipconfig') && argsStr.contains('all')) {
   146│       return dhcpStagedOutput();
   147│     }
   148│ 
   149│     // --- DNS cache ---
   150│     if (exe.contains('ipconfig') && argsStr.contains('displaydns')) {
   151│       return dnsCacheStagedOutput();

   153│ 
   154│     // --- System info / OS info ---

   158│         exe.contains('sw_vers')) {
   159│       return systemInfoStagedOutput(_simulatedPlatform);

   165│     }
   166│ 
   167│     // --- Default fallback ---
   168│     return _defaultStagedOutput(executable, arguments);
   169│   }

## 1. Global Theme & Icons

### `lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart`

**Colors (all `static const Color`):**

| Token | Hex | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| `bgDark` | `#1E1E2E` | Scaffold background |
| `bgSidebar` | `#252538` | Sidebar, surface |
| `bgCard` | `#16213E` | Card backgrounds |
| `accent` | `#E94560` | Secondary accent (red-pink) |
| `accentBlue` | `#0078D4` | Primary accent (blue) |
| `textPrimary` | `#E0E0E0` | Primary text |
| `textSecondary` | `#A0A0B8` | Secondary/muted text |
| `passGreen` | `#4ADE80` | Pass state |
| `warnYellow` | `#FACC15` | Warning state |
| `failRed` | `#EF4444` | Fail/error state |
| `skipGray` | `#888888` | Skipped state |
| `cyan` | `#00BCD4` | Accent highlight / running |

**Typography:** Font family `JetBrainsMono` (monospace).

**ThemeData (`darkTheme`):**
- `brightness: Brightness.dark`
- `scaffoldBackgroundColor: bgDark`
- `fontFamily: 'JetBrainsMono'`
- `colorScheme.dark(primary: accentBlue, secondary: accent, surface: bgSidebar, error: failRed)`
- `AppBarTheme`: background `#1A1A2E`, foreground `textPrimary`, elevation 0
- `CardThemeData`: color `bgCard`, elevation 0, `RoundedRectangleBorder(borderRadius: 12)`

### `lib/core/theme/app_icons.dart`

Uses **Phosphor Icons** (`phosphor_flutter`). Enumerates 40+ icons under `AppIcon` enum:
- Status: `checkCircle`, `xCircle`, `warning`, `warningCircle`, `minusCircle`, `info`
- Navigation: `squaresFour` (dashboard), `activity` (diagnostics), `sliders` (config), `fileText` (report), `gear` (settings)
- Test groups: `cpu` (G1), `shareNetwork` (G2), `globe` (G3), `cloud` (G4), `browser` (G5)
- Actions: `play`, `stop`, `arrowsClockwise`, `x`, `crosshair`, `timer`
- Platforms: `appleLogo`, `androidLogo`, `windowsLogo`, `linuxLogo`, `desktop`, `deviceMobile`, `deviceTablet`, `laptop`
- Misc: `copy`, `share`, `translate`, `envelope`, `gauge`, `wifiHigh`, `cellSignalHigh`, `network`, `lock`, `lockOpen`

Extension `TestStatusIcon` maps `TestStatus` → `AppIcon`.

**Case 3: Has results →** `ListView` (scrollable) with `TestGroupPanel` for each group that has results.

```
├── appBar: AppBar (title: 'NetDiagnostic Simulator', backgroundColor: #1A1A2E, elevation: 2)
│   └── actions:
│       ├── DeviceSelector(selected: device, onChanged: selectDevice)
│       ├── SizedBox(width: 8)
│       ├── IconButton (rotate/orientation toggle, color: white70)
│       └── Container (padding: h6 v4, margin: right 12, borderRadius: 4)
│           └── decoration: BoxDecoration(
│                 color: brandColor@40,
│                 border: Border.all(brandColor@80))
│           └── OsLogo(platform: simulatedPlatform, size: 18, color: white)
│
└── body: LayoutBuilder
    └── InteractiveViewer (minScale: 0.3, maxScale: 2.5, constrained: false, panEnabled: false)
        └── Padding(all: 24)
            └── DeviceFrame(device: device, orientation: state.orientation)
                └── child: _buildAppContent → _SimulatorAppHost
                    └── ProviderScope (overrides: [platformCommandProvider → host native])
                        └── NetDiagnosticApp()    [the full real app]
```

**Key layout/behavior:**
- Auto-scales device frame via `_transformController` and `_updateScale()` on `LayoutBuilder` size changes
- Scale clamped to `[0.3, 2.5]`
- Orientation toggle swaps width/height from `device.screenSize`
- Inner app runs with host-native `PlatformCommand` (real network diagnostics)

```
Padding(v2)
```

### Status Icon (`_buildStatusIcon`)
Renders a `_StatusDot` (essentially `Icon(icon, color, size: 18)`):
- `pass` → `check_circle`, passGreen
- `fail` → `cancel`, failRed
- `warning` → `warning_amber`, warnYellow
- `error` → `error_outline`, failRed
- `skipped` → `remove_circle_outline`, skipGray
- `info` → `info_outline`, accentBlue

### Inline Metric (`_buildInlineMetric`)
```
Container (padding: h5 v1, borderRadius: 3, color: #2A2A4A)
└── Text('${prop.label}: ${prop.value}', 9, #A0A0B8, fontFamily: JetBrainsMono, maxLines: 1)
```

### Property Tile (`_buildPropertyTile`, recursive)
```
Padding(left: depth × 12, top: 2, bottom: 2)
└── Column (crossAxisAlignment: start)
    ├── Row (crossAxisAlignment: start)
    │   ├── Container(6×6, circle, color: severityColor, margin: top 5 right 6)
    │   └── Expanded > RichText
    │       └── TextSpan(style: textSecondary, 11, JetBrainsMono)
    │           ├── TextSpan('${paddedLabel}: ', bold)
    │           └── TextSpan(value, textPrimary)
    └── [if children not empty] ...children.map → recurse _buildPropertyTile(child, depth+1)
```

```
Dialog (backgroundColor: #1E1E2E, borderRadius: 12, border: #3A3A5A)
└── ConstrainedBox(maxWidth: 600, maxHeight: 600)
    └── Column (mainAxisSize: min, crossAxisAlignment: stretch)
        ├── Container(padding: all 16)
        │   └── decoration: border bottom #3A3A5A
        │   └── Row
        │       ├── Icon(description, 20, cyan)
```

### SUMMARY

The codebase follows a clean architecture with domain/engine/presentation layers, featuring 5 diagnostic groups (G1-G5) with platform-specific implementations (Windows/Linux/macOS/Android/iOS) plus a mock simulator, a Flutter Riverpod-based presentation layer with provider state management, and feature modules for dashboard, config, report, and settings screens.

### EVIDENCE
- `/lib/features/diagnostics/domain/entities/diagnostic_result.dart:1-113` — Immutable model for test outcomes

I have all the data. Here is the complete report.

---

### SUMMARY
I recursively explored the entire `/lib/core/` directory and read every file under `core/constants/`, `core/theme/`, `core/router/`, `core/services/`, and the root `logger.dart`. The `core/widgets/` directory is empty — no widget files exist there. The design system is defined across 8 files: a dark theme with Phosphor icon registry, a file-based logger, test ID/group enums, default test targets, a GoRouter with 6 routes, a permission service, and a native FFI diagnostic service wrapper. All file contents were retrieved in full.

### EVIDENCE
- `lib/core/` directory listing: 5 subdirectories (`constants`, `router`, `theme`, `widgets`, `services`) + 1 file (`logger.dart`)
- `lib/core/constants/`: 2 files — `test_ids.dart`, `test_targets.dart`
- `lib/core/theme/`: 2 files — `app_theme.dart`, `app_icons.dart`
- `lib/core/widgets/`: **empty** (no files)
- `lib/core/router/`: 1 file — `app_router.dart`
- `lib/core/services/`: 2 files — `permission_service.dart`, `native_diagnostic_service.dart`
- `lib/core/logger.dart:1-50` — `AppLogger` class: file-based sequential logger writing to `%TEMP%/NetDiagnostic/debug.log` (Windows) or `/tmp/NetDiagnostic/debug.log` (Linux/macOS). Methods: `info()`, `event()`, `error()`, `warn()`. Uses an async queue with `_enqueue`/`_flush`.
- `lib/core/constants/test_ids.dart:1-92` — `TestGroup` enum (g1–g5 with labels and timeouts), `TestId` enum (28 total tests across 5 groups, each with a `labelKey` for localization).
- `lib/core/constants/test_targets.dart:1-30` — `TestTargets` class with static const defaults: `connectivityHost` = `223.5.5.5`, `trustedDns` = `223.5.5.5`, `dnsTestDomains` = `['aliyun.com','qq.com','baidu.com']`, `speedTestUrl` = `https://www.aliyun.com`, `speedTestMinBytes` = 100KB.
- `lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart:1-44` — `AppTheme` class: dark color palette (`bgDark=#1E1E2E`, `bgSidebar=#252538`, `bgCard=#16213E`, `accent=#E94560`, `accentBlue=#0078D4`, plus status colors pass/warn/fail/skip/cyan), `fontFamily='JetBrainsMono'`, `darkTheme` getter returning a `ThemeData` with `ColorScheme.dark`, `AppBarTheme`, and `CardThemeData` (rounded 12px, no elevation).
- `lib/core/theme/app_icons.dart:1-82` — `AppIcon` enum using Phosphor Icons with fill + regular variants. Covers status icons, navigation, test groups, actions, platforms, and misc. Includes `TestStatusIcon` extension mapping `TestStatus` enum to `AppIcon`.
- `lib/core/router/app_router.dart:1-44` — `appRouterProvider` (Riverpod `Provider<GoRouter>`) with `initialLocation: '/diagnostics'` and 6 `GoRoute` entries: `/dashboard`, `/diagnostics`, `/config`, `/report`, `/settings`, `/simulator`. Each maps to a corresponding `Screen` widget from `features/`.
- `lib/core/services/permission_service.dart:1-117` — `PermissionService` singleton checking `Permission.locationWhenInUse` for WiFi tests on Android, with `checkPermissions()`, `canRunDiagnostic()`, and `applyPermissionResult()` to degrade results when permissions are limited.
- `lib/core/services/native_diagnostic_service.dart:1-130` — `NativeDiagnosticService` singleton wrapping C++ FFI (`nd_ffi_bindings.dart`). Maps Dart `TestId` to C `NdDiagnosticId` via `_toNativeId()`. G5 HTTP tests (URL parsing, curl, headers, SSL, redirect, compression, timing) return -1 = Dart-only. `runDiagnostic()` returns `DiagnosticResult?` with fallback to Dart on error or `ND_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED`.

### CHANGES
None.

### RISKS
- `core/widgets/` is empty — UI widget components do not live under `lib/core/widgets/` as expected. Actual reusable widgets may reside in `features/*/presentation/widgets/` or similar feature-level directories, which were not explored per the scope of this task. This could be a project organization mismatch between what the task expected and the actual codebase layout.
- The `speedTestUrl` points to `https://www.aliyun.com` — a homepage download for speed estimation is explicitly noted in the source as unreliable ("NOT a calibrated speed-test payload"), flagged as WARNING below 100KB. This is a known design concession for mainland China accessibility but could produce misleading speed results.
- The `TestId` enum has more entries in `test_ids.dart` (28) than the native mapping in `native_diagnostic_service.dart` (30 cases, but G5 HTTP tests return -1 = Dart-only, plus G5 URL parsing has no case in the switch). This asymmetry could lead to maintenance drift if new tests are added.

### BLOCKERS
None.
